response: Japan, home to a population of 125 million, boasts a GDP of $4.2 trillion with modest growth of 1.1%, alongside an exceptionally low unemployment rate of 2.6% and inflation at 3.2%. Despite its economic strength and impressive life expectancy of 84.6 years, the country grapples with a staggering debt-to-GDP ratio of 263%, while exports remain robust at $756 billion.
